message:
Add input validation assertions and tests for BetaPosterior::new

BetaPosterior::new previously accepted any f64 values for alpha and
beta without validation. A zero, negative, NaN, or infinite parameter
would silently produce nonsensical posterior statistics (mean, variance,
credible intervals) that propagate incorrect speculation decisions
downstream.

Add assertions that alpha and beta are both positive and finite, with
descriptive panic messages that include the offending value. The doc
comment is updated with a # Panics section to document the contract.

Also add four targeted #[should_panic] tests covering the boundary
conditions: zero alpha, negative beta, NaN alpha, and infinite beta.
These ensure the assertions fire correctly and serve as regression
guards against accidentally relaxing the validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
